# Clintela Foundation Design Document

**AI-powered post-surgical patient recovery support**

*Generated by /plan-ceo-review on 2026-03-17 | Mode: SCOPE EXPANSION*

---

## Vision

### 10x Check
Clintela becomes the **operating system for post-acute care**—not just a readmission prevention tool, but the central nervous system for all post-discharge coordination. Every hospital in America uses it. It integrates with every major EHR (Epic, Cerner, Meditech). It has predictive models that identify at-risk patients before they even leave the hospital. It includes a patient mobile app with gamified recovery tracking. It has a marketplace of specialist agents (not just built-in ones). It can handle complex multi-procedure patients, not just single-surgery recovery.

### Platonic Ideal
If the best engineer in the world had unlimited time and perfect taste:

**Patient Experience**: A patient wakes up from surgery. Before they even leave the hospital, Clintela has already sent them a welcome message with their personalized recovery timeline. They get gentle, contextual nudges—"Time for your walk" when their wearable shows they've been sedentary. When they have a question at 2am, they get an instant, accurate answer in their preferred language and communication channel. They never feel alone.

**Clinician Experience**: A nurse opens their dashboard and immediately sees the 3 patients who need attention right now, ranked by urgency. Each patient card shows not just data, but *insight*—"Patient's pain scores trending up, but medication adherence is down—possible undertreatment." One-click actions: schedule follow-up, send message, escalate to physician. The system learns their preferences and surfaces what matters to them.

**Administrator Experience**: Real-time readmission risk across the entire patient population. A/B test different intervention strategies. See which surgeons have better outcomes. Identify systemic issues (e.g., "Patients discharged on Fridays have 2x higher readmission rates").

**Technical**: Event-driven, horizontally scalable, zero-downtime deployments, comprehensive audit trails, HIPAA-compliant by design, with graceful degradation when AI services are unavailable.

---

## Scope Decisions

| # | Proposal | Effort | Decision | Reasoning |
|---|----------|--------|----------|-----------|
| 1 | Patient Mobile App | L | DEFERRED | Web-first lets us validate core experience before native mobile investment |
| 2 | Predictive Risk Scoring | L | DEFERRED | Requires historical data we don't have yet; reactive monitoring delivers immediate value |
| 3 | EHR Integration (Epic/Cerner) | L | DEFERRED | Complex and compliance-heavy; webhook simulation lets us build core system in parallel |
| 4 | Family/Caregiver Portal | M | ACCEPTED | Caregiver involvement directly impacts readmission rates; consent patterns are foundational |
| 5 | Visual Recovery Timeline | S-M | ACCEPTED | Visual progress tracking significantly impacts patient engagement |
| 6 | Medication Photo Verification | M | DEFERRED | Adds complexity (image storage, OCR, HIPAA); text confirmation sufficient for V1 |
| 7 | Multilingual Support | M | ACCEPTED | Language barriers directly impact readmission rates; easier to build i18n from day one |
| 8 | Voice Memos for Clinicians | S-M | ACCEPTED | Natural modality for many patients; transcription workflow reusable for other features |
| 9 | Smart Scheduling (Patient-Preferred) | S | ACCEPTED | Small effort, high engagement; foundational for time-based features. Includes implicit learning from patient behavior and Calendly-quality scheduler for appointments |
| 10 | Offline Mode | M | DEFERRED | Adds complexity (service workers, sync conflicts); online-only acceptable for short-term recovery tool |
| 11 | Marketplace of Specialist Agents | L | DEFERRED | Phase 2+ feature; design agent architecture with clear interfaces that could support plugins later |
| 12 | Gamification / Recovery Milestones | S | ACCEPTED | Low-effort, high-engagement; keep it celebratory and encouraging, not competitive |
| 13 | Patient Portal SSO | M | DEFERRED | Requires partnerships and compliance review; standalone auth lets us move faster |
| 14 | Real-Time Clinician Dashboard (WebSockets) | S-M | ACCEPTED | Minimal additional effort (already using for chat), high clinician value |
| 15 | Dark Mode | S | ACCEPTED | Small effort, high clinician satisfaction; easier to build from start than retrofit |

---

## Accepted Scope

### Patient Interface
- Multi-modal access (SMS, web chat, voice)
- Two-factor authentication (leaflet codes + DOB)
- Family/caregiver portal with consent management
- Visual recovery timeline with milestones
- Multilingual support (i18n framework)
- Voice memos (record, transcribe, summarize)
- Smart scheduling with implicit learning from patient behavior
- Calendly-quality scheduler for appointments
- Recovery milestone celebrations (classy gamification)
- Dark mode support

### Clinician Interface
- Real-time dashboard (WebSockets)
- Color-coded triage view (red/orange/yellow/green)
- Patient summaries with recent developments
- Interaction history and status tracking
- Configurable notifications with escalation chains
- Dark mode support

### Administrator Interface
- Metrics dashboard
- 30-day readmission rates with trend analysis
- Patient population overview and engagement metrics
- Unscheduled escalation tracking
- Complication analytics

### Technical Foundation
- Django web framework (request-based)
- PostgreSQL (relational, document store, caching)
- LangChain/LangGraph multi-agent system
- Supervisor + full specialist agent architecture (placeholders)
- Core agents: Care Coordinator, Nurse Triage, Documentation
- Placeholder specialists: Cardiology, Social Work, Nutrition, PT/Rehab, Palliative, Pharmacy
- Ollama Cloud for LLM (prototyping only, revisit before production)
- WebSocket real-time updates
- Timezone handling: facility default, overridable per patient, UTC internally
- LLM self-evaluation front-line, human QA for edge cases and ongoing review
- Clinical pathways: database-driven templates with per-patient customization

---

## Deferred to Future Phases

### Phase 2
- **EHR Integration (Epic/Cerner)**: SAML prep for clinicians, standalone auth with magic links/SMS for patients. Complex compliance review; webhook simulation enables parallel development.
- **Patient Mobile App (Native iOS/Android)**: Web-first validation before native investment. Short-term usage makes app friction especially costly.

### Phase 3
- **Predictive Risk Scoring**: Requires historical data; AMC partnership will provide. Focus on cardiology patients initially.
- **Medication Photo Verification**: Adds image storage, OCR accuracy, HIPAA image handling complexity. Text-based medication confirmation sufficient for V1.
- **Marketplace of Specialist Agents**: Plugin architecture. Vision of AMCs offering specialist agents to community hospitals.
- **Patient Portal SSO**: Requires partnerships and compliance review. SAML infrastructure prep enables this later.

### Phase 4+
- **Offline Mode**: Service workers, sync conflict complexity. Keep architecture open for future offline support.

---

## Key Technical Decisions

### Authentication
Two-factor via leaflet codes (4-digit) + DOB. Format-flexible DOB parsing, 3-attempt lockout with escalation, caregiver access via same flow.

### LLM Provider
Ollama Cloud for prototyping (revisit before production with HIPAA BAA requirements).

### Agent Architecture
Full supervisor + specialist agent architecture from day one, with placeholder specialists for Cardiology, Social Work, Nutrition, PT/Rehab, Palliative Care, and Pharmacy.

### Clinical Pathways
Database-driven templates with per-patient customization. Patients choose template and make necessary changes during onboarding.

### LLM Testing
Self-evaluation front-line with human QA for low-certainty cases and ongoing retrospective review.

### Timezone Handling
UTC internally, facility timezone as default (overridable per patient), flexible display options including tooltips and abbreviated timezone labels.
